title: Mahamat Idriss Déby Itno
author: @worldbank
text:
Mahamat Idriss Déby Itno

President of the Republic of Chad

This page in:

English

English

Born on April 4, 1984, in N'Djamena, Mahamat Idriss Déby Itno is the current President of the Republic of Chad. He is the son of Marshal Idriss Déby Itno, who led the country from 1990 until his death in April 2021. With a military background, he began his career in the Group of Combined Arms Military Schools (GEMIA) before joining the presidential guard, where he gradually rose through the ranks. He reached the rank of General of the Army in 2021. He has also distinguished himself on the international stage, notably by leading the Chadian Armed Forces during operations in Mali, a commitment that earned him official recognition from the Chadian nation. Following the death of Marshal Idriss Déby Itno in April 2021, during military operations in the north of the country, he was appointed head of the Transitional Military Council, the President of the National Assembly having declined to assume the constitutional interim presidency. This appointment came amidst multiple security challenges and a desire for stability. In May 2024, following a presidential election, he was elected President of the Republic of Chad, a position he has held ever since.
